contact_lookup_status: No named recruiter was visible on the live StepStone posting checked on 2026-03-28. Mercedes-Benz careers contact surfaces point applicants to HR Services, so the letter uses that as the fallback.
job_title: Working student Requirements Engineering for CDCC2.0 Baselayer Software Development
why_company: Mercedes-Benz AG stands out to me because this role combines requirements engineering, software development collaboration, and structured technical analysis in a product environment where precision and systems thinking matter.
job_description: Mercedes-Benz AG is hiring a working student in Requirements Engineering for CDCC2.0 Baselayer Software Development in Sindelfingen. The role supports requirement analysis and documentation, collaboration with development teams, preparation of artifacts for software development, and structured communication in a technical environment.
requirements: Studies in computer science, business informatics, software engineering, or a comparable field
Interest in requirements engineering and software development
Strong analytical and structured working style
Ability to collaborate with technical teams
Confident knowledge of German and English, written and spoken
Interest in documentation, coordination, and software-related analysis
cv_about_override: Business analysis professional building stronger technical depth in requirements engineering, systems-oriented work, and structured software delivery.
cv_summary_override: Experienced business analysis and product professional with a strong foundation in requirements, workflow mapping, stakeholder alignment, and structured execution. Alongside my earlier business background, I am deepening my technical path through Software Engineering studies at Heilbronn University and Business Information Systems studies at Vietnamese-German University. My work across SQL, automation, digital operations, and cross-functional delivery has strengthened my interest in requirements engineering and technical coordination in structured software environments.
cover_letter_opening: I am applying for the position of Working student Requirements Engineering for CDCC2.0 Baselayer Software Development at Mercedes-Benz AG. This role stands out to me because it combines structured requirements work, technical coordination, and software-focused collaboration in exactly the direction I want to develop further.
cover_letter_body_one: In my previous roles across digital products and operations-heavy environments, I worked closely with stakeholders, designers, engineers, and business teams to structure requirements, document workflows, and support delivery. That experience gave me a strong foundation in turning business and user needs into clear, usable artifacts for implementation.
cover_letter_body_two: Through my current Software Engineering studies at Heilbronn University, my Business Information Systems background, and my hands-on work across SQL, automation, and digital operations, I have been intentionally moving toward more technical and systems-oriented work. This makes me particularly interested in a working student role where I can contribute to requirements engineering in a real software development context.
cover_letter_motivation: I am especially interested in this role because CDCC2.0 Baselayer Software Development appears to require the combination of structured analysis, technical communication, and implementation support that I have been building across both study and work.
cover_letter_closing: I am available to start from 01 May 2026 and can work up to 20 hours per week in line with my current student visa. While my German is not yet at a confident professional level, I work confidently in English at C1 level with IELTS 7.5 and continue to improve my German. If this specific role is not the best fit at the moment, I would be grateful to be considered for another suitable working student opportunity within Mercedes-Benz. I would welcome the opportunity to discuss my application in an interview.
